content.full_text:
March 16, 1990

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: CONFIRMATION OF ADVICE REGARDING USE OF EXCERPTS OF LEGISLATIVE PUBLICATIONS

Dear Ms. Donovan:

This will confirm our telephone conversation of late yesterday in which you advised me that the Commission staff has altered its views on the subject of use of excerpts from legislative publications which could be sent on their entirely under 18901(b)(10) in "mass mailings."

You indicated that with the time to engage in further discussion, the staff felt that restriction on the use of such excerpts should await some evidence of abuse. I have conveyed that information to the Senate Rules Committee. As a result, it is unnecessary to have a further meeting on the subject or to bring the matter before the Commission.

As you know, the specific publication in question is designed to increase citizen participation in the legislative process. To do so, the publication includes copies of excerpts from actual legislative publications to show citizens how to use those publications to follow and comment on legislation of interest. Printing and dissemination of that publication may now go forward.

I wish to thank you and your staff for the prompt attention which you have given this matter. I recognize that the Commission's staff is working under difficult time constraints, and that such time constraints may result in a cautious approach when new issues arise. All of us who must live with these new and complex laws are under a great deal of pressure. Consequently, I am pleased that this matter has been resolved without the need to expend additional resources. I hope that an informal working

relationship may be maintained, so that we may mutually strive to make this law more workable for all concerned.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

ROBERT E. LEIDIGH

REL/kl
cc: Senate Rules Committee
     Assembly Rules Committee
